2604.13692.title: Breaking the Generator Barrier: Disentangled Representation for Generalizable AI-Text Detection
2604.13692.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) generate text that increasingly resembles human writing, the subtle cues that distinguish AI-generated content from human-written content become increasingly challenging to capture. Reliance on generator-specific artifacts is inherently unstable, since new models emerge rapidly and reduce the robustness of such shortcuts. This generalizes unseen generators as a central and challenging problem for AI-text detection. To tackle this challenge, we propose a progressively structured framework that disentangles AI-detection semantics from generator-aware artifacts. This is achieved through a compact latent encoding that encourages semantic minimality, followed by perturbation-based regularization to reduce residual entanglement, and finally a discriminative adaptation stage that aligns representations with task objectives. Experiments on MAGE benchmark, covering 20 representative LLMs across 7 categories, demonstrate consistent improvements over state-of-the-art methods, achieving up to 24.2% accuracy gain and 26.2% F1 improvement. Notably, performance continues to improve as the diversity of training generators increases, confirming strong scalability and generalization in open-set scenarios. Our source code will be publicly available at https://github.com/PuXiao06/DRGD.
2604.13692.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）生成的文本越來越類似於人類寫作，區分AI生成內容與人類撰寫內容的微妙線索變得越來越難以捕捉。依賴於生成器特定的工件本質上是不穩定的，因為新模型迅速出現，降低了這些捷徑的穩健性。這使得未見的生成器成為AI文本檢測中的一個核心且具挑戰性的問題。為了應對這一挑戰，我們提出了一個逐步結構化的框架，將AI檢測語義與生成器感知工件分離。這是通過一種緊湊的潛在編碼來實現的，該編碼鼓勵語義最小化，隨後進行基於擾動的正則化以減少殘餘糾纏，最後是一個區分適應階段，將表示與任務目標對齊。在MAGE基準上的實驗涵蓋了7個類別中的20個代表性LLMs，顯示出對最先進方法的一致改進，實現了高達24.2%的準確率增益和26.2%的F1改進。值得注意的是，隨著訓練生成器的多樣性增加，性能持續改善，證實了在開放集場景中的強大可擴展性和泛化能力。我們的源代碼將在https://github.com/PuXiao06/DRGD上公開。
2604.13692.author: Xiao Pu et.al.
2604.13692.authors: Xiao Pu, Zepeng Cheng, Lin Yuan, Yu Wu, Xiuli Bi
